critical.mandate: PRD must exist before starting UX design
Every design decision must trace to a user need from the PRD

step.title: Load PRD
step.action: Read {planning_artifacts}/prd.md
Extract user personas, user journeys, and functional requirements

step.title: User Personas
step.action: Refine persona definitions from PRD
Add: scenarios, goals, tech proficiency, accessibility needs

step.title: Information Architecture
step.action: Design sitemap and navigation structure
Define content hierarchy and page relationships
Map each page or section to the FR IDs it serves — every page must trace to at least one FR. Flag any user-facing FR from the PRD that has no corresponding page in the sitemap.

step.title: Wireframes
step.action: Create text-based wireframe descriptions for key screens
Define layout, component placement, interaction patterns
Annotate each wireframe with the FR IDs it addresses. Flag any FR with user-facing behavior that has no wireframe representation.

step.title: Interaction Patterns
step.action: Define common UI patterns used across the application
Specify component library or design system choices
Document form behaviors, validation, error states
Map each interaction flow to the corresponding user journey from the PRD. Every PRD user journey must have a defined interaction pattern.

step.title: Accessibility
step.action: Define WCAG compliance targets (A, AA, AAA)
Plan keyboard navigation, screen reader support
Define color contrast and text sizing standards

step.title: Figma MCP Detection and Mode Selection
step.action: Probe for available Figma MCP server. Load figma-integration.md detection section JIT to determine if a design tool adapter is available.
Present mode selection to user: [Generate] Create Figma frames alongside ux-design.md | [Import] Import existing Figma designs (read-only) | [Skip] Text-only UX spec, no Figma integration
Skip Figma integration — proceed with text-only UX design output. Log: "No Figma MCP server detected. Generating markdown-only ux-design.md."

step.title: Generate Mode — UI Kit Page Creation

critical.mandate: Generate mode is the ONLY mode that performs write operations to Figma (FR-140). All other modes are read-only. Write operations: create pages, create frames, create component instances. Minimum required Figma API scopes: files:read + file_content:read + files:write.

step.action: Load figma-integration.md frames section JIT from _gaia/dev/skills/figma-integration.md
Create a UI Kit page named "UI Kit — Generated" in the Figma design file via MCP
Extract design tokens from {planning_artifacts}/design-system/design-tokens.json (W3C DTCG format)
Create color styles with semantic aliases from token definitions (e.g., color.surface.primary, color.text.primary)
Create typography styles from composite token specs (heading-1, heading-2, body, caption)
Create spacing grid tokens and base components with state variants (default, hover, active, disabled, focus)
Error handling: on HTTP 429 rate limit, perform a single retry after backoff. If 429 persists, graceful fallback to markdown-only flow — log warning and skip remaining Figma operations.

step.title: Generate Mode — Per-Screen Frame Generation
step.action: Parse PRD user journeys to determine screens needing frames

For each screen, generate frames at 6 viewports: 280px (foldable inner, FR-174), 375px (mobile), 600px (foldable outer, FR-174), 768px (tablet portrait), 1024px (tablet landscape, FR-174), 1280px (desktop)
Compose frames using UI Kit components from the previous step — apply auto-layout with responsive constraints from component specs
Label frames using naming convention: {ScreenName}/{Viewport} (e.g., Dashboard/Desktop, Login/Mobile)
Collect all generated Figma node IDs for downstream recording in ux-design.md

step.title: Generate Mode — Prototype Flow Setup
step.action: Map PRD user journey steps to the generated screen frames
Create prototype flow connections between frames via Figma MCP, linking screens in the order defined by each user journey
Validate flow completeness: every user journey step must map to a generated frame. Flag any unmapped journey steps as warnings.

step.title: Generate Mode — Asset Export Configuration
step.action: Configure PNG export at 1x, 2x, 3x densities for raster assets (images, illustrations)
Configure SVG export for icon components

Generate platform-specific asset catalogs per FR-175: iOS .xcassets catalog structure with Contents.json manifests, Android drawable-mdpi/drawable-hdpi/drawable-xhdpi/drawable-xxhdpi/drawable-xxxhdpi directories
Write export configuration and asset manifest to {planning_artifacts}/design-system/assets/ directory
Check {project-path}/.figma-cache/ for cached responses (1h TTL) before making MCP read calls — use cached data if fresh, fetch and cache if stale or missing

step.title: Generate Mode — Record Figma Node IDs and Enhance ux-design.md
step.action: Build Screen-to-Frame mapping table from all generated Figma node IDs: columns Screen Name | Viewport | Figma Node ID | Page
Add figma: YAML frontmatter block to ux-design.md containing: file_key, pages array with node IDs for each generated page, and last_synced ISO 8601 timestamp
Add Design Tokens reference section linking to {planning_artifacts}/design-system/design-tokens.json with token category summary
Add Component Inventory section listing all generated UI Kit components with their Figma node IDs, variant counts, and state definitions
Add Screen-to-Frame mapping table with all generated frames across all viewports
Ensure backward compatibility: existing text-only ux-design.md content is preserved. Figma sections are additive — they appear after the standard UX design sections.

step.title: Generate Output
step.template-output: Generate UX design document with: personas, information architecture, wireframe descriptions, interaction patterns, component specifications, accessibility plan, FR-to-Screen Mapping table (FR ID | Screen/Page | Wireframe Section). If Generate mode was active: include figma: frontmatter block, Design Tokens section, Component Inventory with Figma node IDs, and Screen-to-Frame mapping table.

step.title: FR-140 Compliance Check
step.action: Audit all MCP calls executed during Generate mode: classify each as READ (get_file, get_styles, get_components, get_images, get_frames) or WRITE (create_frame, create_component_instance, create_page)
Verify FR-140 constraint: write operations only occurred during Generate mode steps (8–12). Import mode and Skip mode must have zero write calls.
Document minimum required Figma API scopes: files:read + file_content:read (default for all modes), files:write (Generate mode only)

step.title: Optional: Accessibility Review
step.ask: Would you like to review the UX design for WCAG 2.1 accessibility compliance? This spawns a subagent in a separate context. Recommended for user-facing applications. (yes / skip)
step.action: If yes: spawn a subagent using the Agent tool: "Load {project-root}/_gaia/core/tasks/review-accessibility.xml. Read its entire contents. Target: {planning_artifacts}/ux-design.md. Follow the flow steps EXACTLY. Generate accessibility findings report."
If skip: accessibility review can be run anytime later with /gaia-review-a11y

next-step.primary: Review UX design for WCAG 2.1 accessibility compliance

alternative.context: If accessibility review will be done later
